# Packages

A short stack does most of the work in this book. `ggplot2` [@R-ggplot2] is the
foundation; everything else either builds figures on top of it or supplies the
modeling output we plot.

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(ggplot2)
library(hvtiPlotR)        # house plots, themes, and PPT/PDF export
library(hvtiRutilities)   # data dictionaries, labels, manifests
library(gt)               # publication tables
library(patchwork)        # compose multi-panel figures
```

What each one is for:

- **hvtiPlotR** [@R-hvtiPlotR] -- the core of the book. It wraps the figures we
  publish into a two-step API: an `hv_*()` constructor validates the data and
  returns an object, and `plot()` turns that object into a bare ggplot. It also
  carries the house themes (`theme_hv_manuscript()`, `theme_hv_poster()`, the
  PowerPoint themes) and the save helpers.
- **hvtiRutilities** [@R-hvtiRutilities] -- the data-governance side: data
  dictionaries, variable labels, dataset manifests, and the synthetic-data
  generators we use so the examples run without touching real patient data.
- **ggRandomForests** [@R-ggRandomForests], **randomForestSRC**
  [@R-randomForestSRC; @randomForestSRC2007; @randomForestSRC2008], and **varPro**
  [@R-varPro] -- the random-forest stack. `randomForestSRC` fits the forest,
  `varPro` does variable selection, and `ggRandomForests` turns either one into a
  ggplot. These load in the random forest chapters where they are used.
- **TemporalHazard** [@R-TemporalHazard] -- our R port of the SAS/C HAZARD
  module, for parametric and nonparametric hazard modeling. It loads in its own
  chapter.
- **gt** [@R-gt] -- publication-quality tables. We use it directly for now; a
  companion `hvtiRtables` package is planned to give tables the same house
  treatment the plots get.
- **patchwork** [@R-patchwork] -- composes several plots into one figure with
  shared labels and panel tags.
- **survival** [@R-survival; @survival-book] -- the `Surv()` objects and
  Kaplan-Meier machinery underneath the survival figures.

You do not need all of these for any single figure. Each chapter loads what it
uses in its first chunk.
